data.body:
PASS 1 plan-review (T10 — Infrastructure / IaC overlay). The overlay APPLIES: this plan provisions real cloud infra as Terraform (EC2, dedicated EBS data volume, EIP, IAM roles/policies, DLM, SNS/CloudWatch alarms, S3 remote state, a GitHub ruleset). Verdict: strong across all seven sub-checks; ONE grounded coaching finding (stateful-volume + snapshot encryption-at-rest, which is both a secrets and a blast-radius concern).

AFFIRMATIONS (T10 dimensions this plan satisfies):
- (a) STATE + plan-before-apply: remote S3 backend with S3-native `use_lockfile` locking (no local state, no DynamoDB) guarded by `required_version >= 1.10` (versions.tf:1-19 confirms the floor + the "< 1.10 silently ignores use_lockfile" warning); a one-time LOCAL-state bootstrap breaks the bucket chicken-and-egg (bootstrap/main.tf, documented + `prevent_destroy` on the state bucket + versioning + SSE + public-access-block). `terraform plan`-before-`apply` discipline is reaffirmed epic-wide, specifically for the high-stakes S6 ruleset replace, and lives in S7's runbook AC.
- (b) LEAST-PRIVILEGE IAM: no wildcard admin. SSM read scoped to the `arn:...:parameter/rebar/prod/*` prefix (iam.tf:58-61, explicitly NOT Resource "*"); `kms:Decrypt` constrained by a `kms:ViaService` condition (iam.tf:63-72); the replication deploy key is single-repo WRITE-only and the sole ruleset bypass; the two `resources=["*"]` statements are either condition-bounded (kms) or the unavoidable CloudWatch `PutMetricData`/logs actions. IAM-role ownership contract (S1 CREATES the role; S2/S4a only ADD separately-named scoped inline policies) prevents over-grant/clobber. No long-lived creds in IaC (SSM SecureString + `__BOT_TOKEN__` placeholder substituted at boot).
- (c) IDEMPOTENCY & DRIFT: drift is explicitly reasoned about — single-owner contracts for the EC2 role, `gerrit.config` (S2 base + S3 `[change]`-only read-modify-write, not full-file overwrite), and the DLM policy (S1 owns; S7 only ASSERTS via a `check` block, monitoring.tf:186-191); configs are rendered/committed IaC under infra/, not hand-edited; `ignore_changes=[ami]` (main.tf:69-71) keeps a newly-published SSM AMI id from force-replacing the running box on every apply.
- (d) BLAST RADIUS / destroy-replace safety: `prevent_destroy=true` on the stateful NoteDb data volume (main.tf:27-40), the EIP (main.tf:90-96) and the state bucket; the data volume is deliberately SEPARATE from root so a destroy/replace can't silently take Gerrit data; freeze-and-restore posture + "merged code always safe on the GitHub mirror" bound RPO; a single-env PoC (no dev/stage/prod matrix needed, explicitly accepted).
- (e) SECRETS: AWS SSM Parameter Store SecureString pulled to a `chmod 600` `.env` at boot; the enumerated param set is completeness-checked (`/rebar/prod/alert-endpoint` + `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`); no plaintext secret committed. (See the one finding for the at-rest gap on the volume that HOLDS a secret.)
- (f) COST & SIZING: cost-lean choices are explicit and justified — single Graviton t4g.large (~8GB), Gerrit heap pinned ~2GB, gp3, default VPC, NO ALB/NAT, SSM Parameter Store (free) over Secrets Manager, on-box nginx+Let's Encrypt; DLM retain=7 bounds snapshot storage.
- (g) OBSERVABILITY & TEARDOWN: concrete metric (Rebar/Gate:GerritReachable), alarm (< 1 for 2×5min, treat_missing_data=breaching), EC2 System/Instance status-check backstop alarms, SNS topic + SSM-sourced email endpoint (monitoring.tf); reproducible as-code under infra/ with a rebuild-from-IaC + non-destructive restore drill (S7); teardown of the bootstrap state bucket is documented as an explicit state-migration procedure (bootstrap/main.tf:11-15).

FINDING (1):

- [ ] Specify `encrypted = true` (+ a KMS/CMK) for the dedicated `/var/gerrit` NoteDb EBS data volume AND the root device, so it and its inherited DLM snapshots are encrypted at rest. This is a T10(d)/(e) stateful-resource + secrets concern (independently of the T5c reviews): the volume is the plan's designated `prevent_destroy` stateful resource AND it stores `refs/meta/config`, which per ADR-0014 holds the live webhook bot token — so an unencrypted stateful volume is a plaintext-secret-at-rest gap, and its DLM snapshots (backup.tf) inherit the source volume's absent encryption. The plan's "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet / AC1 name only "a dedicated gp3 EBS volume ... with prevent_destroy + DLM daily snapshots" and never state encryption; code confirms the gap — `aws_ebs_volume.data` (main.tf:27-40) sets `type=gp3` + `prevent_destroy` but declares NO `encrypted` argument, and a repo-wide `encrypted` search under infra/ returns ZERO matches (so even root_block_device is unencrypted, contra derisk note E1's "gp3-encrypted" dry-run claim, which covered only the root device shape).
  - criteria: [T10]
  - location: Decomposition & resolved design questions -> "NoteDb persistence + backup" bullet; AC1; infra/terraform/main.tf:27-59, infra/terraform/backup.tf
  - evidence: plan phrase "NoteDb on a dedicated gp3 EBS volume at `/var/gerrit` with `prevent_destroy` + DLM daily snapshots (S1)"; ABSENCE of any `encrypted`/KMS statement for the data volume or root device in the plan text; code fact — `aws_ebs_volume.data` (main.tf:27-40) and `root_block_device` (main.tf:56-59) declare no `encrypted`, and `search infra/ 'encrypted'` -> 0 hits; ADR-0014: the bot token lives in `refs/meta/config`, i.e. on this volume (T10(e) no plaintext secrets at rest; T10(d) stateful-resource safety).
  - suggested_fix: Add `encrypted = true` (AWS-managed SSM/EBS key or a project CMK) to `aws_ebs_volume.data` and `root_block_device`, and add a line to S1's volume AC stating the encryption propagates to the DLM snapshots so the token-bearing `refs/meta/config` is encrypted at rest.

No other T10 defects — state/locking, least-privilege IAM, idempotency/drift ownership, blast-radius `prevent_destroy`, secrets-manager sourcing, cost/sizing, and observability/teardown are all specified and code-corroborated.
